name: NOWA

intro.headline: She walks in covered head to toe and the room still turns.
intro.body: NOWA makes abayas for young women who never once saw modesty as a limit. They came to us with a name they loved and nothing to put it on. No logo, no colours, no rules. We built them a brand that feels expensive before anyone gets close enough to read a price tag.

statement: You can dress quiet and still take up the whole room.
statementSupport: That one line decided the gold, the spacing, and everything we left out.

logo.headline: We swapped the O for a moon and left the rest alone.

construction.headline: Small change. Took forever.
notes: The moon sits exactly where the O used to. Same width, same weight, same rhythm. You read the word first and catch the moon a second later. That order is the whole trick.
That curve ate most of the project. Too thick and it stopped being a letter. Too thin and it disappeared the moment we shrank it. We kept redrawing until one version survived both.
Gold foil, twelve millimetres, stitched into the neck of an abaya. If it holds up there it holds up anywhere.

concept.headline: Everything else we tried felt borrowed. The moon didn't.

typography.headline: Luxerie for the moments that matter. DM Sans for the other ninety percent.
typography.alt: Display Luxerie. Everything else DM Sans, four weights. Scale built on 1:1.618, from a 60pt headline down to 15.3pt body.

colour.headline: Gold, dialled back until it stopped looking like money and started looking like light.

graphic.headline: Icons drawn with the same pen as the logo.

card.headline: Thick uncoated card, gold foil, nothing else. You feel it before you read it.

stationery.headline: The boring stuff, treated like it matters.

social.headline: On a phone screen everything is shouting. This one just sits there.

website.headline: A lot of white space and one gold mark holding it all down.

environment.headline: Blow it up to four metres and the moon still does all the work.
